The BAMPFA is fun to visit for their rotating exhibits and close proximity to the UC Berkeley campus and downtown Berkeley. We visited recently to see the very well done exhibit of African American quilts. Typically when a museum shows quilts (or other textiles) one is not able to touch them. This is understandable. They found a way to allow for that tactile experience by having small examples of the quilt techniques that were attached to the wall and yet could be touched. This was such a neat idea that I wondered why more museums didn't adopt this practice. It enhanced the experience. I also liked that on the day we went they were appraising, recording, and photographing quilts from the community to add to their database. This shows a strong desire to be part of the community and to connect the exhibit to the quilters in the area. I appreciated that level of engagement. 

The museum is near plenty of restaurants and coffee shops. We brought a picnic with us and so walked up to the Berkeley campus and enjoyed relaxing on the grassy open space. 

Well worth a visit when an exhibit strikes your fancy.

It’s a nice space with rotating art exhibitions. Berkeley students are free and can bring in one guest. They have other discounts admission options such as for veterans which was a nice touch.  It’s worth a stroll through if you have the time
